description: OpenAPI SDK — Collect per-service specs, merge into unified spec, generate C# SDK via Kiota, wire into Blazor client

tasks.title: Collect and merge OpenAPI specs from all services
tasks.prompt:
All 8 SNAPP services already expose OpenAPI specs via /openapi/v1.json. Create tooling to collect and merge them.

1. Create scripts/collect-openapi.sh that:
   a. Ensures Docker Compose is running (all service containers up)
   b. For each service (auth, user, network, content, intelligence, transaction, notification, linkedin):
      - Fetches http://localhost:8000/api/{service-path}/openapi/v1.json via Kong
      - If a service doesn't expose the spec at that path, try http://localhost:{direct-port}/openapi/v1.json directly
      - Saves to api/specs/{service}.json
   c. Reports which specs were collected successfully

2. Create src/Snapp.Tools.SpecMerge/ as a .NET console app that:
   - Reads all JSON specs from api/specs/
   - Merges into a single OpenAPI 3.1 document:
     - Combines all paths (prefixed by service if needed)
     - Deduplicates shared schemas (DTOs from Snapp.Shared appear in multiple specs)
     - Adds unified security scheme: Bearer JWT
     - Adds server URLs: http://localhost:8000/api (dev), https://api.{domain} (prod placeholder)
     - Sets info.title = 'SNAPP API', info.version from git tag or '0.1.0'
   - Outputs api/snapp-api.json and api/snapp-api.yaml
   - Validates the merged spec is well-formed

3. Create scripts/merge-openapi.sh that:
   a. Runs collect-openapi.sh
   b. Runs dotnet run --project src/Snapp.Tools.SpecMerge
   c. Reports success/failure and endpoint count

4. Add Swagger UI volume mount or config to serve the merged spec at http://localhost:8090

Test: run merge-openapi.sh, verify api/snapp-api.json exists and contains endpoints from all 8 services.
tasks.done_when: collect-openapi.sh fetches specs from all services. SpecMerge tool produces valid merged api/snapp-api.json. Swagger UI serves it.

tasks.title: Generate C# SDK via Kiota
tasks.prompt:
Generate a typed C# SDK from the merged OpenAPI spec using Microsoft Kiota.

1. Install Kiota if not present: `dotnet tool install --global Microsoft.OpenApi.Kiota` (or use the dotnet tool manifest)

2. Create scripts/generate-sdk.sh that:
   a. Runs merge-openapi.sh first (ensures spec is current)
   b. Runs Kiota to generate the SDK:
      kiota generate \
        --language CSharp \
        --openapi api/snapp-api.json \
        --output src/Snapp.Sdk/Generated \
        --class-name SnappApiClient \
        --namespace-name Snapp.Sdk \
        --exclude-backward-compatible
   c. The generated code goes into src/Snapp.Sdk/Generated/

3. Update src/Snapp.Sdk/Snapp.Sdk.csproj:
   - Add Kiota dependencies: Microsoft.Kiota.Abstractions, Microsoft.Kiota.Http.HttpClientLibrary, Microsoft.Kiota.Serialization.Json, Microsoft.Kiota.Authentication.Azure (or a custom Bearer provider)
   - Reference Snapp.Shared (for shared DTOs if Kiota can use them)

4. Create src/Snapp.Sdk/Authentication/BearerTokenProvider.cs:
   - Implements Kiota's IAuthenticationProvider
   - Accepts an access token string (or a Func<Task<string>> token provider)
   - Attaches Authorization: Bearer {token} to all requests

5. Create src/Snapp.Sdk/Extensions/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:
   - AddSnappSdk(this IServiceCollection services, Action<SnappSdkOptions> configure)
   - Registers SnappApiClient in DI with HttpClient + BearerTokenProvider
   - SnappSdkOptions: BaseUrl, TokenProvider

6. Verify: `dotnet build src/Snapp.Sdk/` compiles with zero errors

7. Write a simple test in test/Snapp.Sdk.Tests/ that instantiates SnappApiClient and verifies it has the expected API surface (auth, users, networks, content, intel, tx, notif, linkedin properties).
tasks.done_when: Kiota generates SDK from merged spec. Snapp.Sdk builds. BearerTokenProvider works. DI extension registers correctly. SDK test passes.

tasks.title: Wire SDK into Blazor client
tasks.prompt:
Integrate Snapp.Sdk into Snapp.Client so the Blazor app uses the generated typed SDK for API calls.

This is a refactoring task — the Blazor client currently uses hand-written HttpClient service wrappers. Replace them with the Kiota-generated SnappApiClient where possible.

1. Add project reference: Snapp.Client → Snapp.Sdk

2. Update Snapp.Client/Program.cs:
   - Register Snapp.Sdk via AddSnappSdk()
   - Configure BaseUrl from appsettings.json (ApiBaseUrl)
   - Configure TokenProvider from AuthState (reads JWT from localStorage)

3. Create Snapp.Client/Services/SnappSdkWrapper.cs:
   - A thin wrapper around SnappApiClient that handles:
     - Token refresh on 401 (get new token from AuthState, retry)
     - Error mapping (Kiota exceptions → user-friendly error types)
   - Injected as a scoped service

4. Update ONE page as a proof-of-concept — choose the simplest one (e.g., Profile/MyProfile.razor):
   - Replace the hand-written HttpClient call with SnappSdkWrapper
   - Verify it works the same way
   - Add a comment: // TODO: migrate remaining pages to SDK

5. Do NOT migrate all pages — this is a proof-of-concept. Future sprints will migrate the rest.

6. Verify: `dotnet build snapp.sln` compiles. The migrated page works correctly. bUnit tests still pass.
tasks.done_when: Snapp.Sdk wired into Blazor DI. One page migrated as proof-of-concept. Build clean. Existing bUnit tests pass.

tasks.title: Sprint 14.5 validation
tasks.prompt:
Run exactly these steps:

1. `scripts/merge-openapi.sh` — verify merged spec generated
2. `scripts/generate-sdk.sh` — verify SDK generated
3. `dotnet build snapp.sln` — record pass/fail
4. `dotnet test snapp.sln --verbosity quiet` — record per-project counts
5. Verify api/snapp-api.json exists and contains paths from all 8 services (grep for /api/auth, /api/users, /api/networks, /api/content, /api/intel, /api/tx, /api/notif, /api/linkedin)
6. Write .claude/reports/sprint-14.5-validation.md with results
7. `git add -A && git commit -m '[S14.5-004] Sprint 14.5 validation'`

Do NOT investigate failures, fix code, or do manual walkthroughs.
tasks.done_when: Validation report written. Merged spec has all 8 service paths. SDK builds. All tests pass. Committed.
